title: AAR — Franchise honors banner (division titles, wild cards, pennants, championships)
scope: On the team page, show a banner of franchise accolades across seasons — each with the years won — once a save has simmed past season 1. Four honors, using the game's canonical playoff names.

rationale.item: `playoff_series` is wiped at rollover (season_archive._reset_for_next_history_season), and `seasons` persists only the single overall champion_abbrev per season — so pennants and wild-card berths are not recoverable for past seasons; the only way to track all four going forward is a small capture at archive time, while the bracket still exists.
Design reference was a Viperball team page: a small filled pill under the team's record line, all inside the hero banner.
Why not the literal MLB spec from the request: same reasoning as the standings change — the honor set is config-driven; no hardcoded two-league shape.

actions.description: New season_team_honors table in SCHEMA (one row per team per season that earned an honor; flags for four accolades plus year/season_number); existing saves pick it up on next boot via executescript

_snapshot_team_honors(season_id, season_number, year) called from archive_current_season right after _snapshot_standings, before the bracket is wiped; reads live teams, playoff_series, and playoffs.champion()

backfill_team_honors() reconstructs division titles and overall champion for archived seasons with no honors rows; pennants/wild cards stay 0 (unrecoverable); _ensure_team_honors_backfilled() one-time lazy backfill guarded by a sim_meta flag
_team_honors(team) aggregates the table into per-honor year lists, picks the canonical series label by league count, passed to team.html
honors-banner row of pills under the hero record: label, ×N count, years; scoped CSS, colour-coded per honor (gold / indigo / green / blue)
backfill_honors subcommand (mirrors existing backfill_* commands) for an explicit run

risk_posture: The "League Champion" pill is suppressed in single-league universes (where the league final *is* the title), and the overall-title label switches between "World Series Champion" and "Champion" by league count — no hardcoded two-league shape.
The lazy backfill writes on first team-page view of an un-migrated save; it is idempotent and flag-guarded.

validation_claims.claim: py_compile clean across all four modules; team.html and standings.html parse.
Fresh-DB init_db() creates season_team_honors with the expected columns.

End-to-end test on a seeded 8-team / 2-league DB with two archived seasons and a live bracket: capture produced the correct division winners, the single correct wild card, both pennant winners, and the World Series champion; backfill reconstructed division titles and overall champions for the two prior seasons (pennants/wild cards correctly left empty). Example output — ALA: Series 2027 · League 2027 · Division 2025·2026·2027; ALB: Wild Card 2027 only.
validation_claims.check_method: Re-run the seeded end-to-end capture/backfill scenario

non_actions.reason: this is the o27v2 live app team page, where the playoff data lives. A bundle-independent version could follow.

residual_risks: Pennants and wild-card berths are not recoverable for past seasons — the bracket is gone; backfilled seasons leave them 0.
open_hooks: A bundle-independent (almanac) version of the honors banner could follow.
section_headers_original: AAR — Franchise honors banner (division titles, wild cards, pennants, championships)
The constraint that shaped the design
Why not the literal MLB spec from the request
unmapped_content: Honor definitions: Division Title — finished 1st in its (league, division). Wild Card — reached the playoff field as a non-division-winner. League Champion — won its league final (series_kind = 'championship'). World Series Champion (or Champion in a single-league universe) — the overall title (world_series winner, or the lone league's champion). No season filter on playoff_series is needed — only the current bracket is ever present. Franchise identity is the stable team_id across seasons within a save, with team_abbrev as the fallback for backfilled rows.
parser_notes: No date anywhere in document; marked undated (checked headings and body).
